title: Green success tokens, amber warning banners, raw input replacements, and NotesEditor semantic colors
description: Continuation of the systematic UI improvement loop. This batch fixes remaining hardcoded green/amber colors scattered across components, replaces a raw readonly input with shadcn Input, creates a shadcn Checkbox component, creates a shadcn Progress component, and fixes semantic color tokens in NotesEditor and GoalProgress.

userStories.title: Fix hardcoded green success colors across ResumeFitSection, DetailPanelBody, SettingsReferralSection, Tags
userStories.description: As a developer, green-* classes used for success/matched states across four components should use consistent semantic tokens instead of hardcoded Tailwind greens.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/ResumeFitSection.jsx, bg-green-100 text-green-700 dark:bg-green-900/40 dark:text-green-400 on matched skill badges replaced with bg-primary/10 text-primary dark:bg-primary/20
In src/components/DetailPanelBody.jsx, text-green-600 dark:text-green-400 on checklist completion icon replaced with text-primary
In src/components/SettingsReferralSection.jsx, text-green-500 on CheckCircle icon replaced with text-primary
In src/pages/Tags.jsx, text-green-600 on save button icon replaced with text-primary
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read each file before editing. These are all success/positive signal uses of green — map them to text-primary (brand color) rather than a hypothetical 'success' token that doesn't exist yet. Make minimal targeted changes only.

userStories.title: Fix FilterBar ArchivedBanner and DetailPanelBody overdue warning amber colors
userStories.description: As a developer, inline amber warning banners in FilterBar and DetailPanelBody use hardcoded amber-* classes. Replace with semantic tokens consistent with the banner fixes in prd_47.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/FilterBar.jsx ArchivedBanner, bg-amber-50 border-amber-200 text-amber-800 dark:bg-amber-900/20 dark:border-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300 replaced with bg-muted border-border text-foreground

In src/components/DetailPanelBody.jsx overdue warning section, amber-50/amber-700 hardcoded classes replaced with bg-muted/border-border pattern or bg-destructive/5 border-destructive/30 text-destructive if it represents an error state
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read both files before editing. The ArchivedBanner in FilterBar is a status indicator — bg-muted text-foreground is appropriate. For DetailPanelBody overdue warning, if it's a deadline warning use bg-destructive/5 text-destructive, if it's informational use bg-muted.

userStories.title: Fix NotesEditor hardcoded rose/amber character counter colors
userStories.description: As a developer, the NotesEditor character counter uses hardcoded text-rose-600 and text-amber-600 for warning/error states. These should use semantic tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/NotesEditor.jsx, text-rose-600 (at/over limit) replaced with text-destructive

text-amber-600 (approaching limit) replaced with text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400 OR replaced with a warning semantic — acceptable to keep amber-600 with a dark variant added if no warning token exists
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/NotesEditor.jsx first. The rose over-limit state maps cleanly to text-destructive. For the amber near-limit state, adding a dark:text-amber-400 variant is acceptable if no semantic warning token exists.

userStories.title: Fix GoalProgress hardcoded stroke-green-500 to semantic token
userStories.description: As a developer, GoalProgress SVG ring uses a hardcoded stroke-green-500 for completion state. Replace with semantic primary token.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/GoalProgress.jsx, stroke-green-500 replaced with stroke-primary
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/GoalProgress.jsx first. Single targeted change — the ring going green on completion maps to brand primary color.

userStories.title: Replace raw readonly <input> in SettingsProfileSection with shadcn Input
userStories.description: As a developer, the email display input in SettingsProfileSection uses a raw <input> with hardcoded className. Replace with shadcn Input component for consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/SettingsProfileSection.jsx, raw <input id='email-display' type='email' readOnly className='border border-border...'> replaced with <Input type='email' value={...} readOnly disabled />
Import Input from src/components/ui/input (check the actual path — may be input.jsx or similar)
The hardcoded className on the old input removed
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/SettingsProfileSection.jsx first to confirm the exact line and import path. Check src/components/ui/ for the Input component filename before importing.

userStories.title: Create shadcn Checkbox UI component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable shadcn-style Checkbox component at src/components/ui/checkbox.jsx backed by @radix-ui/react-checkbox, so raw <input type='checkbox'> elements can be replaced.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/components/ui/checkbox.jsx exists and exports a named Checkbox function
Uses @radix-ui/react-checkbox as the primitive (CheckboxPrimitive.Root, CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator)

Default className: h-4 w-4 shrink-0 rounded-sm border border-primary shadow focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 data-[state=checked]:bg-primary data-[state=checked]:text-primary-foreground
Renders a Check icon from lucide-react/dist/esm/icons/check inside the indicator
Check @radix-ui/react-checkbox is already in package.json before importing — if not present, skip this story and mark passes: true with a note that the dependency is missing
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read package.json first to verify @radix-ui/react-checkbox is available. If it is, implement. If not, set passes: true in the PRD with a note 'skipped — dependency not in package.json' and do NOT run npm install.

userStories.title: Replace raw <input type='checkbox'> in FilterBar with shadcn Checkbox
userStories.description: As a developer, the filter bar checkboxes use raw <input type='checkbox'> elements without custom styling. Replace with shadcn Checkbox for visual consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/FilterBar.jsx, raw <input type='checkbox' checked={...} onChange={...}> replaced with <Checkbox checked={...} onCheckedChange={...} />
Import Checkbox from src/components/ui/checkbox
Checkbox component must exist (US-523 creates it)
Checked state still controlled correctly
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: shadcn Checkbox uses onCheckedChange (not onChange). The checked prop works the same. Read FilterBar.jsx first to understand the filter state pattern.

userStories.title: Fix OfferComparison winner badge hardcoded amber colors
userStories.description: As a developer, the winner badge in OfferHeaderCell uses hardcoded bg-amber-100 text-amber-800 dark:bg-amber-900/40 dark:text-amber-300 classes. These are intentional amber-for-winner styling but should have proper dark mode variants.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/pages/OfferComparison.jsx OfferHeaderCell, verify bg-amber-100 text-amber-800 dark:bg-amber-900/40 dark:text-amber-300 already has correct dark mode variants
If dark mode variants are missing, add them
If they are already present and correct, mark this story complete with no changes needed
hover:bg-amber-100 on the non-winner state replaced with hover:bg-primary/10 hover:text-primary
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/pages/OfferComparison.jsx OfferHeaderCell component first. The amber winner badge is intentional and acceptable. Focus on the non-winner hover state and any missing dark mode variants.
